entry.message: Sandbox evaluated dm message from sandbox.user.

input.message: Marin hizo dos tareas con prompt similares de martin. Deberias de armar un mensaje pidienod que martin se una al war room para alinear con el.

decision.reason: The user is reporting a duplicate prompt issue between two contributors (Marin and Martin) and asking me to send a message asking Martin to join the War Room to align. This is a valid community management request supported by the project guidelines, which recommend using the War Room for coordination and alignment.
decision.reply: Thanks for flagging this. We recommend that Martin join the War Room to align with Marin on these similar prompts and avoid duplication. The War Room is open Monday through Friday from 11:15 AM to 7:00 PM ARG. Once inside, please join the breakout room called Stargazer - Team. Here is the link: https://scale.zoom.us/j/91510346485?pwd=IEPPxvhcHt1W25AXq01eMC3Ynn5SsO.1#success
guidelineSnippets:
As part of the onboarding process, you will submit a quality assessment.
Please reach out to the project team in the Zoom War Room after completing the quality
assessment. This step is mandatory for you to begin tasking. The project team will need
to perform a manual review to ensure that you passed the quality assessment and to
provide access to Cursor.
Environment Setup (return to TOCÓÇÇ
Before getting started with the project, both Cursor and Docker must be properly set up
and running. Here is a quick guide to make sure your environment is properly set up.
Cursor
‚óè Follow this link and download the 2.6 cursor version. If you already have Cursor
installed, uninstall it and install this specific version, without this version, the models
won ºt work.
‚óè Even if you already have access to the Cursor Team Plan, reach out to the Zoom
War Room and ask any of the QMs for a new plan, as it must be renewed for this
project.
‚óè Go to the Key Manager: https://cursor-intelligence-service.outlier.ai/key-manager
‚óè Click on View API Key
‚óè Open Cursor Settings ‚Üí Models
‚óè Disable all models, and add:
‚óã claude-qwen3ÓÇà5ÓÇà27b-scale
‚óã claude-4.6-sonnet-scale
‚óè Enable OpenAI API Key; paste in your API Key and Override Base URL with
https://cursor-intelligence-api.outlier.ai/api/v1
‚óè Click "Verify".
‚óè Test these models in Cursor now.
Docker
‚óè Make sure Docker is installed and up to date. If you don't have it yet, follow this
guide.
‚óè Ensure Docker has enough RAM and disk space to run containers smoothly.
‚óè Clean up unused images and containers regularly to free up space and avoid build
errors.
Project Structure (return to TOCÓÇÇ

‚óè Think ahead. Before writing, plan how the gold solution and tests will look. If you
cannot picture them, the prompt is not ready.
2.2 Rate the difficulty of your issue (return to TOCÓÇÇ
Rate your issue by counting how many of the following complexity signals apply:
1. Spread: The fix changes 3 or more files.
2. Cross-module interaction: The fix affects more than one part of the project.
3. Architectural awareness: To fix it, you need to understand how the project is
organized.
4. Non-trivial logic: The fix needs decision-making in the code, not just renaming,
moving, or deleting something.
5. Implicit edge cases: The solution should also handle special cases or possible
errors.
6. Regression risk: A bad fix could accidentally break something else.
Difficulty Signals that apply
Easy 1 - 2
Medium 3 - 4
Hard 5ÓÇù
2.3 Create the Issue (return to TOCÓÇÇ
Location: App/
‚óè For all issue types except Bug Injection, the files inside app/ must remain identical
to the original repo.
‚óè For Bug Injection, introduce the bug directly inside the app/ folder so that this
modified version represents the faulty instance state that the solution will fix.
2.3.1 Generate Base-to-Instance Patch
You must generate an additional patch called basetoinstance.patch. If the issue type is
Bug Injection, after introducing the bug, you need to capture the exact diff between the
working base state and the broken instance state

Changelog
Date Updates
June 10 ‚Üí NEWÓÇí Step 0 - Project Onboarding
May 28 ‚Üí model_trace.patch is now required for model failure trace
Project Overview
The purpose of this project is to create a database of challenging issue requests for a
repository. This database will be used to benchmark and improve the ability of coding
agents to implement changes in a codebase in response to user prompts.
To achieve this, you will build a standard project setup around an assigned repository. You
will then create a challenging prompt requesting changes to the codebase. You will check
that the model fails to respond adequately to your prompt, write your own solution to the
issue, create unit tests to verify your solution, and compose a rubric that can be used to
grade how successful the agent was in responding to your issue. When you are finished,
you will prepare the required patches, scripts, and Docker files so the whole workflow can
be reproduced and validated later.

Task Overview
Description
Onboarding &
Installations
Before tasking for the first time, you must complete the project
onboarding and reach out to the project team after completing your
quality assessment. Then you will set up Cursor and Docker. You
only need to do this once!
Setup and
Analysis
First, you will clone the assigned repo and set up your environment.
You will also explore the codebase so that you understand its
structure and functionality.
Issue Creation
and Solution
You will write a prompt that asks the agent to make changes to the
codebase based on an assigned issue (e.g., bug fixes or new
features). You will make sure that your prompt is challenging
enough that at least one model fails to solve it. Then, you will write
your own Golden Solution to your prompt.
Testing
You will create unit tests that can check whether a given solution
adds all of the requested functionality without breaking any
previously existing functionality.
Docker and
Scripts
You will prepare Dockerfiles and scripts that ensure that your work
on the task is reproducible.
Validation Script You will run a validation script that checks that your solution passes
all of the unit tests that you wrote.
Add Tags You will add relevant tags to your task to categorize it.
Rubric Creation You will write a rubric that consists of a series of criteria that can
grade how good or bad a response to your prompt is.
Evaluation Script
Before submission, you will run an evaluation script on your task.
The suite checks the quality and alignment of your prompt, tests,
rubrics, gold patch, Dockerfiles, and granularity.
Step 0ÓÇí Onboarding & Installations (return to TOCÓÇÇ
Project Onboarding (return to TOCÓÇÇ
Before your first task, you will be guided through our onboarding process, where you will
learn the basics of what you need to know to succeed on the project.
